_description: Governed decodes for structural terms used in LENS / Tier-2 / Workspace surfaces. Registry-driven — no hardcoded text on bound surfaces.
_inference_prohibition: ACTIVE — decodes are descriptive only; no advisory or causal content

entries.short_decode: Signal value identified as an outlier relative to values observed across this run's entity set.
entries.long_explanation: Activation determined by comparing this signal's value to the distribution of equivalent signals across all assessed entities in the current run. The signal is activated when its value deviates significantly from the run-internal norm. No external baseline is applied.

entries.short_decode: Signal value assessed against a theoretical reference baseline rather than run-observed values.
entries.long_explanation: Activation determined by comparing this signal's value to a defined theoretical expectation or threshold. No run-relative comparison is made. The signal is activated when its value falls outside the theoretically acceptable range for this structural dimension.

entries.short_decode: Zone contains multiple co-active conditions from structurally independent dimensions within the same domain scope.
entries.long_explanation: A COMPOUND_ZONE designates a diagnostic zone where two or more structurally independent conditions are simultaneously active within the same domain. Compound zones indicate multi-dimensional pressure convergence — distinct condition types are co-present, not a single condition expressing at higher severity.

entries.short_decode: A governed diagnostic unit where active signals and conditions concentrate structural pressure within a domain segment.
entries.long_explanation: A PRESSURE_ZONE is a governed analytical unit identifying a domain segment where one or more active conditions create concentrated structural pressure. Pressure zones are the primary unit of Tier-2 diagnostic scope and are derived from the projection layer, not inferred from topology alone.

entries.short_decode: The active conditions originate within this zone's own domain scope.
entries.long_explanation: PRIMARY attribution indicates that the conditions active in this zone originate structurally within the zone's own domain. The domain is the structural home of the identified conditions, not a secondary recipient of propagated pressure from an adjacent or upstream domain.

entries.short_decode: Pressure propagates into this zone from an upstream or adjacent domain; conditions are not locally originated.
entries.long_explanation: SECONDARY attribution indicates that the conditions active in this zone are structurally sourced from another domain and propagated into this zone's scope. The domain exhibits the conditions but is not the structural origin point. Attribution profile is determined by the projection layer.

entries.short_decode: The numeric range within which the canonical score is structurally supported by available evidence.
entries.long_explanation: The CONFIDENCE_BAND defines the lower and upper bounds of the assessment score's structural confidence. A band of 60–100 means the available evidence structurally supports a score no lower than 60 and no higher than 100. The band narrows as evidence coverage increases.

entries.short_decode: Classification of the breadth of structural evidence available for this assessment.
entries.long_explanation: EVIDENCE_SCOPE describes whether the assessment draws on complete or partial structural evidence. FULL indicates all domains are grounded with no unresolved structural conditions. PARTIAL indicates one or more domains have unresolved conditions that limit the completeness of the analysis. BOUNDED indicates significant structural coverage gaps.

entries.short_decode: No advisory interpretation, root cause claims, or remediation recommendations may be derived from this surface.
entries.long_explanation: INFERENCE_PROHIBITION is an active governance constraint. All data on this surface is structural and evidential only. No advisory content, causal inference, root cause determination, or remediation guidance may be produced from or attributed to data on this surface. Presence of INFERENCE_PROHIBITION: ACTIVE is mandatory on all Tier-2 and Workspace surfaces.

entries.short_decode: Count of diagnostic zones that have at least one active signal bound to their scope.
entries.long_explanation: SIGNAL_COVERAGE measures how many of the identified diagnostic zones have at least one PSIG signal active within their domain scope. Zones without signal coverage cannot be structurally characterized beyond their topological classification. Signal coverage is a measure of diagnostic depth, not structural health.

entries.short_decode: Proportion of assessed domains for which structural evidence is fully resolved.
entries.long_explanation: STRUCTURAL_COVERAGE indicates what proportion of the assessed domain space has complete structural grounding. Domains labeled GROUNDED have full structural evidence and no unresolved conditions. WEAKLY GROUNDED domains have one or more unresolved structural conditions that limit the completeness of structural characterization for that domain.

entries.short_decode: Degree to which a diagnostic finding can be followed back through the evidence chain to its originating artifact.
entries.long_explanation: TRACE_CONTINUITY classifies whether a zone's findings can be traced through the complete evidence chain. FULLY_TRACEABLE means the signal → condition → zone chain is intact and all links are verifiable. PARTIALLY_TRACEABLE means one or more links are present but the chain is incomplete. NOT_TRACEABLE means the chain cannot be verified from current evidence artifacts.
